sub: What this elective module is about — and what is expected of you

title: About the Lecturer
desc: Aviation and software — a lifelong combination

body.caption: First contact with aviation technology: the motorized ultralight my father built with his own hands, 1986.

items: In information technology since the age of five — decades as backend developer, lead and architect
Universities, banks, insurance, government, automotive, transport networks, startups, medical — and aviation safety: exactly the mix air traffic systems demand

title: What This Lecture Covers
desc: From regulation to running code: the full stack of drone air traffic

items: Introduction — live flight tracking, drone regulation, the EU drone regulation 2024
Your project — a hands-on drone-software build you propose, implement and defend; the BlueSky competition is one route you can take
The DJI lab — a one-button app already flies the Mini 4 Pro from our own code; you build on it
Software safety standards — DO-178C, ED-109, ED-153, and why 'perfect' software can still kill
Aeronautical information exchange — NOTAM, METAR, AIXM, ASTERIX, OLDI
Efficient control & monitoring — UTM, automated fleet control, sensor fusion
Technologies — OpenAPI, Reactive Java (Spring WebFlux), message queues, CQRS, Karate test automation
Simulation & data — BlueSky simulation environments and real-time GIS mapping
Conclusion & outlook — future drone technology, AI & autonomous systems, international standards

title: Course Organization & Grading
desc: Your graded deliverable: a hands-on drone-software project

rows: Build your own drone-software idea on top of the working client

items: Scope your project early — narrow and working beats broad and unfinished
Build on the working client — a small, real capability the base app does not have yet
The project counts towards the final grade — expectations are spelled out in Module 2
